selected_title: Evaluating the military's technology absorptive capacity
question: What mechanism does Madeleine Jacobs identify in “Evaluating the military's technology absorptive capacity” that deepens the evidence from “How Can Firms’ Basic Research Turn Into Product Innovation? The Role of Absorptive Capacity and Industry Appropriability”?

evidence_spans.text: The Department of Defence, in the light of the rapid advances in military technologies, requires continuous evaluation of the processes, structures and policies that facilitates the transfer of advance technologies to end-users. It further requires an evaluation of the readiness of the end-users and their ability to exploit these technologies to provide them with a competitive advantage in combat. The question is asked: “Are the end users ready to absorb the new technology?” and “Are the end users able to exploit the new technology to achieve combat superiority?” These questions were addressed through the evaluation of the absorptive capacity of the South African military at a particular point in time. learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Absorptive capacity, Exploit through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 186 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
